evals.prompt: Pipa find work from these messy lead notes and tell me what deserves action now.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the find-work lane and returns opportunity, why now, fit/urgency/confidence, owner, next action, source, and unknowns.
assertions: Does the output select `find work` as the primary Pipa lane?
Does it include opportunity, why now, fit or urgency, confidence, owner, next action, and source or `TBD`?
Does it avoid claiming live CRM, inbox, Slack, Reddit, or web access unless a connected tool was actually used?

evals.prompt: Pipa define work from this client thread. Scope is fuzzy and acceptance checks are missing.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the define-work lane and creates a working brief with scope, out-of-scope, acceptance checks, open questions, decisions, and source gaps.
assertions: Does the output select `define work` as the primary Pipa lane?
Does it include working brief, in scope, out of scope, acceptance checks, open questions, decision needed, and source or `TBD`?
Does it avoid routing to a live voice huddle unless voice wording is explicit?

evals.prompt: Pipa deliver work: what is blocked, who owns it, and what needs escalation today?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the deliver-work lane and returns current state, blockers, owner gaps, next actions, escalation trigger, sources, and unknowns.
assertions: Does the output select `deliver work` as the primary Pipa lane?
Does it include current state, blockers or risks, owners, next actions, due or review dates, status, evidence/source, and `TBD` unknowns?
Does it avoid creating automation from status/update wording unless the user asks for recurring or event-driven behavior?

evals.prompt: Pipa get paid: which invoices or billable-time items need action before Friday?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the get-paid lane and returns money items, amount/account/project if known, status, risk, owner, due/review date, source, and follow-up.
assertions: Does the output select `get paid` as the primary Pipa lane?
Does it include money item, amount/account/project or `TBD`, status, risk, recommended follow-up, owner, due/review date, and source?
Does it avoid claiming payment, invoice, banking, payroll, or time-record access unless connected tools were used?

evals.prompt: Pipa grow relationships: who needs a check-in and what should I say?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the grow-relationships lane and returns relationship signal, why now, suggested touch, owner, timing, source, and watchlist.
assertions: Does the output select `grow relationships` as the primary Pipa lane?
Does it include relationship needing attention, why now, suggested touch, owner, timing, source, and watchlist?
Does it avoid scheduling a reminder or sending a message unless the user explicitly asks for that connected capability?

evals.prompt: Pipa learn from the work from this completed project so we can reuse the lesson next time.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the learn-from-the-work lane and returns lessons, evidence, next-time change, owner, reuse location, and follow-ups.
assertions: Does the output select `learn from the work` as the primary Pipa lane?
Does it include lesson, evidence, what changes next time, owner, reuse location or `TBD`, and follow-ups?
Does it avoid declaring closure without acceptance, handover, or archive evidence?

evals.prompt: Pipa plan this onboarding work. Requirements are scattered, timeline pressure is high, and ownership is fuzzy.
evals.expected_output: Supports the old plan wording by reframing it into the define-work lane.
assertions: Does the output route old `plan` wording into `define work`?
Does it avoid presenting lifecycle commands as the primary public model?
Does it preserve requirements, baseline, decisions, owners, and acceptance checks as possible outputs?

evals.prompt: Pipa initiate this new delivery project so we have reusable context, stakeholders, risks, and a plan-ready handoff.
evals.expected_output: Supports old initiate wording by reframing it into the define-work lane.
assertions: Does the output route old `initiate` wording into `define work`?
Does it preserve project context, stakeholders, risks, and plan-ready handoff checks?
Does it avoid exposing lifecycle routes as the public command surface?

evals.prompt: Pipa execute this delivery week: coordinate active work packages, owners, handoffs, and follow-through.
evals.expected_output: Supports old execute wording by reframing it into the deliver-work lane.
assertions: Does the output route old `execute` wording into `deliver work`?
Does it choose one primary delivery action path rather than exposing lifecycle routes as the public model?
Does it preserve owners, next actions, due/review dates, status, evidence/source, and `TBD` unknowns?

evals.prompt: Pipa monitor this delivery week and tell me where we're stuck.
evals.expected_output: Supports old monitor wording by reframing it into the deliver-work lane unless money or relationship intent dominates.
assertions: Does the output route old `monitor` wording into `deliver work`?
Does it include current state, blockers, owners, next actions, evidence/source, and unknowns?
Does it avoid exposing lifecycle routes as the public command surface?

evals.prompt: Pipa close this out. Signoff happened, but support handoff and archive readiness are unclear.
evals.expected_output: Supports old close wording by reframing it into the learn-from-the-work lane.
assertions: Does the output route old `close` wording into `learn from the work`?
Does it evaluate signoff, handover, lessons, archive readiness, and unknowns?
Does it avoid implying final closure when handoff or archive evidence is missing?

evals.prompt: Pipa help
evals.expected_output: Returns the six operating lanes plus concise connected-capability notes.
assertions: Does the output list `find work`, `define work`, `deliver work`, `get paid`, `grow relationships`, and `learn from the work`?
Does it keep connected capabilities separate from the six core lanes?
Does it avoid making lifecycle commands the first screen?

evals.prompt: Pipa. From the conversation above, we have messy client notes, fuzzy scope, and missing acceptance checks for the onboarding work.
evals.expected_output: Routes by conversation context into the define-work lane instead of showing the command menu.
assertions: Does the output route to `define work` from conversation context even though no explicit command was supplied?
Does it avoid showing the generic help/menu as the primary response?
Does it preserve scope, acceptance checks, open questions, decisions, and source gaps?

evals.prompt: Pipa audio brief this requirements doc so I can listen to it on my phone.
evals.expected_output: Routes to pipa-audio-brief and preserves source extraction, audio generation, publishing, and blocker behavior.
assertions: Does the output route to `pipa-audio-brief` because the user explicitly asked for audio/listenable output?
Does it preserve source-readability blockers instead of producing a degraded audio brief?
Does it preserve the standalone workflow's generation and publishing contract?

evals.prompt: Pipa brief this PR before standup.
evals.expected_output: Does not route to pipa-audio-brief because the user did not ask for audio/listenable/spoken output.
assertions: Does the output avoid selecting `pipa-audio-brief`?
Does it treat `brief` as a generic define-work or deliver-work request unless audio/listenable wording is present?

evals.prompt: Pipa voice session so I can talk this plan through while walking.
evals.expected_output: Routes to pipa-huddle-beta and preserves live voice-session scope, transport/privacy blockers, and optional handoff behavior.
assertions: Does the output route to `pipa-huddle-beta` because the user explicitly asked for live voice conversation?
Does it avoid routing to `pipa-audio-brief`?
Does it preserve the standalone workflow's transport, privacy, and optional handoff contract?

evals.prompt: Pipa talk through this plan in writing and turn it into scope, acceptance checks, and next decisions.
evals.expected_output: Stays inside the define-work lane because the user did not ask for a live voice session.
assertions: Does the output avoid selecting `pipa-huddle-beta`?
Does it route written planning/talk-through wording to `define work`?
Does it avoid treating `talk through` alone as live voice intent?

evals.prompt: Pipa follow up with the client about the delayed milestone.
evals.expected_output: Routes to grow-relationships or deliver-work and does not schedule a self-email reminder because no future email reminder was requested.
assertions: Does the output avoid selecting `pipa-follow-up-reminders`?
Does it treat client follow-up as relationship or delivery follow-through?
Does it avoid claiming a reminder was scheduled?

evals.prompt: Pipa remind me by email next Friday at 9 to chase this invoice.
evals.expected_output: Routes to pipa-follow-up-reminders because the user asked for a specific future email reminder to themself.
assertions: Does the output route to `pipa-follow-up-reminders`?
Does it preserve one-shot reminder scope, timezone resolution, verified self-recipient guardrail, and confirmation before create?
Does it avoid turning this into recurring automation or inbox monitoring?

evals.prompt: Pipa deliver work every Friday at 9 with a Slack status update.
evals.expected_output: Routes to pipa-triggers only after recognizing the recurring delivery request and preserves trigger proposal confirmation before create.
assertions: Does the output route recurring/event-driven delivery to `pipa-triggers` as a connected capability?
Does it require event/source/delivery details and final trigger proposal confirmation before create?
Does it keep the lane objective visible instead of flattening the work into generic automation?

evals.prompt: Pipa deliver work: give me a weekly status update now with blockers and next owner actions.
evals.expected_output: Produces a one-time deliver-work status update and does not route to pipa-triggers because future recurring delivery was not requested.
assertions: Does the output avoid selecting `pipa-triggers`?
Does it treat `weekly` as the reporting horizon when no recurring automation wording is present?
Does it avoid creating or proposing automation without an explicit recurring/event-driven request?

evals.prompt: Pipa find work from Gmail and Linear, then create a GitHub issue for the best opportunity.
evals.expected_output: Routes through Composio discovery/schema rules for live external app access and does not invent tool slugs or write externally without confirmation.
assertions: Does the output route external app access/action through `composio`?
Does it require tool discovery or verified tool information before execute?
Does it avoid inventing Composio tool slugs or claiming live records before fetching them?

evals.prompt: Pipa find work from these pasted lead notes and recommend the next outreach angle.
evals.expected_output: Stays in the find-work lane and does not route to Composio because the source material is already supplied and no live external app action was requested.
assertions: Does the output avoid selecting `composio`?
Does it work from the pasted source material instead of claiming live app access?
Does it avoid inventing external app records, tool slugs, or fetched sources?

evals.prompt: Pipa get paid: review billable time records for this week and summarize what can be invoiced.
evals.expected_output: Routes to pipa-time-tracking for explicit time-record review while preserving the get-paid objective and keeping unsupported invoicing/payroll claims out of scope.
assertions: Does the output select `pipa-time-tracking` for explicit time-record review?
Does it avoid claiming invoice or payroll support from time tracking alone?
Does it preserve the get-paid money objective?

evals.prompt: Pipa get paid: review invoice risk and margin for this project from the summary below.
evals.expected_output: Stays in the get-paid lane and does not route to pipa-time-tracking because no time-entry operation was requested.
assertions: Does the output avoid selecting `pipa-time-tracking`?
Does it treat invoice risk and margin review as `get paid` work?
Does it avoid claiming time-record access when the prompt only supplied a summary?

evals.prompt: Pipa frobnicate this delivery plan.
evals.expected_output: Avoids inventing a frobnicate workflow and routes by the clear delivery-plan context when safe; otherwise shows help with one clarification path.
assertions: Does the output avoid inventing a `frobnicate` workflow?
Does it route by the clear delivery-plan context or show the six operating lanes only if routing is unsafe?
Does it ask at most one clarifying question if needed?
